name: Banffshire (United Kingdom, historic)
description: An orange field over a white band over a blue base; a white blazing sun in the upper hoist, and a row of orange-and-white roundels (whisky-barrel tops) along the base.
history: Registered with the Flag Institute on 28 October 2023 as the flag of the historic county of Banffshire. Orange recalls the county's sunsets and agriculture; the white band its many bridges; the blue base its rivers and the sea; and the barrel-top roundels the whisky distilled from its waters.

features.role: orange field above a white band over a blue base
features.symbolism: orange for sunsets and agriculture; the white band for the county's bridges; blue for its rivers and the sea

features.role: white blazing sun in the upper hoist
five roundels (orange over white) along the base — the tops of whisky barrels
features.symbolism: the whisky distilled from the county's waters; orange for the amber spirit
